title: Properties & Methods
description: Adding data and behavior to classes.

slides.title: Adding Behavior 🏃
slides.content: Classes wouldn't be very useful if they only held data. We can attach functions directly inside the class body, known as Methods.

slides.title: Class Methods
slides.content: Define a method simply by writing its name and parameters. No `function` keyword is required!

quizData.question: What is the crucial keyword used inside class methods to reference the properties belonging to that exact specific object?
quizData.explanation: `this` always points to the instance of the object running the code. So `this.name` gets *that* dog's name.

slides.title: State Management 📊
slides.content: Methods are frequently used to modify the properties (the 'state') of the object internally, rather than having external code mess with the properties directly.

slides.title: Updating State
slides.content: Provide controlled ways to alter data.
